topic: Verdict taxonomy — do the six categories still make sense after 7 weeks of substantive content change?
change_summary: Operator-commissioned Phase 2 rerun (HOL-TAXONOMY, commissioned 2026-05-28T05:55Z). Step-back assessment after ~7 weeks of content evolution since the c1 holistic pass on 2026-04-06. Verdict distribution has shifted substantially: Self-Contradicted 12→20 (+67%), Misleading 23→16 (-30%), Std Model Explains 8→18, Unfalsifiable 4→3, Not Demonstrated 3→3, Refuted by Data ?→12 (data/wins.json:72-total).
overall_assessment: The six categories remain conceptually sound and the framework is materially stronger than at c1. The new Evaluation Guide section 'Verdict Assignment Criteria' (build-scripts/generate-html.js:1714-1736) explicitly addresses the three biggest c1 holes — the Misleading/Std Model decision boundary, the 'rigged taxonomy' / no-positive-category objection, and the primary-issue rule for multi-failure WINs — with a stated decision rule rather than implicit practice. Most c1 reclassification recommendations were applied: WIN-054 moved Misleading→Self-Contradicted (a stronger move than c1's recommendation of Not Demonstrated), WIN-043/060 had their duplication language stripped from findings to match the primary-issue framework, and WIN-023's finding now cites Campuzano 2019 archaeomagnetic records to address the kernel-of-truth gap. However, three new application-consistency holes have surfaced as the WIN set grew: (a) the 20 Self-Contradicted WINs include 3 clusters of multiple WINs that share single root causes (Schumann ~22 Hz contradiction across 4 WINs, bounded-geometry cosmological contradiction across 4 WINs, dome H(r)/coupling-law contradictions across 3-4 WINs) — but only the tidal cluster is acknowledged in the counting-methodology paragraph of the Evaluation Guide; (b) WIN-068 (Eclipse magnetic ensemble, added since c1) is the only post-cleanup Std Model Explains finding that still surfaces 'Duplicates WIN-X' in its one-line finding, reintroducing the inconsistency c1 forced cleanup on; (c) the 'b' suffix convention used by WIN-057b and WIN-058b is undocumented and visually collides with their non-'b' counterparts. None of these is fatal — the taxonomy survives as a working framework. But all three give a careful dome defender a process objection that was specifically anticipated and partially addressed in the new Verdict Assignment Criteria section.

holes_found.description: Self-Contradicted cluster inflation parallels the dome's own count-inflation pattern we criticize. Of 20 Self-Contradicted WINs (data/wins.json), at least 11 collapse to ~4 independent geometric root causes: (1) Schumann/cavity-frequency cluster — WIN-002, 029, 038, 061 all reduce to 'dome's H(r)=8537·exp(−r/8619) gives ~22 Hz, not 7.83 Hz'; (2) Cosmological-bounded-geometry cluster — WIN-047 (low-z Hubble), 048 (CMB Axis of Evil), 052 (RAR lensing), 054 (El Gordo) all reduce to 'bounded firmament has no spatial framework for objects at z>0'; (3) Dome H(r) / coupling-law cluster — WIN-044 (FSF version-unstable), 056 (solar elevation), 067 (Antarctic gravity hole) reduce to 'dome's stated H(r) parameters produce values radically different from observations'; (4) The acknowledged tidal cluster — WIN-045/046/049/050/051 reduce to 'local moon at 2,534 km cannot produce semidiurnal tides' (Evaluation Guide already acknowledges this one). The Evaluation Guide's counting-methodology paragraph (generate-html.js:1733) names ONLY the tidal cluster as an example of where 'all derive from one underlying geometric impossibility' — but the same logic applies to the Schumann, cosmological, and H(r) clusters. A dome defender will correctly observe: 'You criticize me for inflating one INTERMAGNET observation into 5 WINs, then count one geometric impossibility (dome cavity → 22 Hz) as 4 self-contradictions. By your own counting standard, you have 8-10 independent self-contradictions, not 20.' This is rhetorically the most exploitable inconsistency in the current taxonomy. The data field detail_group exists (used for tidal, eclipse-magnetic, and other clusters) but is empty for the Schumann SC cluster (002/029/038/061 all detail_group:NONE) and the cosmological SC cluster (047/048/052/054 all detail_group:NONE), so the structural data already shows the gap.
holes_found.recommendation: Two complementary edits: (1) In the Evaluation Guide counting-methodology paragraph (around build-scripts/generate-html.js:1733), generalize the acknowledgement: keep the tidal cluster as the worked example, then add 'Other Self-Contradicted clusters share single geometric roots: WIN-002/029/038/061 all derive from the dome cavity's ~22 Hz floor; WIN-047/048/052/054 all derive from the bounded firmament's inability to accommodate galaxies at z>0; WIN-044/056/067 all derive from the published H(r) parameters producing values incompatible with observation. We count each WIN as the dome counts it — the underlying logical units are fewer.' (2) Populate detail_group on the Schumann/cosmological/H(r) clusters in data/wins.json so the structural data matches the prose acknowledgement. This preempts the strongest dome-defender process objection without changing any verdict.

holes_found.description: WIN-068 (Eclipse magnetic ensemble, 39 eclipses) is the only post-cleanup Std Model Explains finding that mentions duplication in its one-line finding. Current finding: 'Non-discriminating: the Chapman–Sq ionospheric framework ... Duplicates WIN-010 and WIN-025 observational basis at a larger aggregation level' (data/wins.json:WIN-068). Per the c1 holistic recommendation, WIN-043 and WIN-060 had similar 'duplicate of WIN-X' language stripped from their findings to align with the Verdict Assignment Criteria's primary-issue rule (build-scripts/generate-html.js:1718-1729): when a WIN's primary failure is non-discrimination, secondary duplication is informational and belongs in the detail, not the finding one-liner. WIN-068 was added or modified after that cleanup and reintroduces the inconsistency. A dome defender comparing WIN-043 ('does not mention duplication') against WIN-068 ('does mention duplication') will reasonably ask why two structurally identical cases receive different treatment in the headline finding.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) move 'Duplicates WIN-010 and WIN-025 observational basis at a larger aggregation level' from the finding to detail_verdict_text, matching the WIN-043/060 cleanup, OR (b) document explicitly why WIN-068's duplication is structural enough to belong in the headline (e.g., 'Aggregation duplication is qualitatively different from per-station duplication — the ensemble itself is the same evidence at a coarser grain'). Recommend (a) for consistency with the existing cleanup; (b) is defensible but requires an additional taxonomy distinction the framework does not currently support.

holes_found.description: WIN-058 (Unified angular coordinate θ = −lonE) verdict is Misleading but the finding leads with non-discrimination, not the structural-mishandling failure the Misleading definition requires. Current finding (data/wins.json:WIN-058) opens with 'θ = −lonE is a relabeling of the centuries-old east-longitude convention — any polar-azimuthal projection of the globe satisfies it automatically.' This is a textbook Std Model Explains framing per the Verdict Assignment Criteria (generate-html.js:1722-1724): 'the only problem is that standard physics predicts the same result without dome geometry.' The detail does mention curve-fitting (V12 0.9941 EW scale factor persisting in distance_analysis.py) and WGS-84 input dependency, which justify Misleading. But the finding one-liner — what readers see in the summary table — frames the WIN as non-discriminating relabeling, not as curve-fitting or data mishandling. A reader of the summary table will perceive a mismatch between the verdict and the headline reason given.
holes_found.recommendation: Two options. (a) Rewrite the finding to lead with the Misleading-qualifying structural problem: 'V12's 0.9941 EW scale factor persists live in distance_analysis.py, so the V13 angular identity coexists with the V12 distance-scaling factor without reconciliation; the coordinate system also requires WGS-84 longitude as input (OPEN-001) and achieves 7.3% error vs. Vincenty's sub-0.01%. The θ = −lonE identity itself is relabeling of standard east-longitude convention.' (b) Reassign to Std Model Explains and surface the curve-fitting/V12 issue in WIN-057 (Two-zone disc topology, already Misleading for curve-fitting). Recommend (a) — the V12/V13 reconciliation issue is genuine and justifies Misleading, but the finding text needs to surface it.

holes_found.description: Not Demonstrated category remains too narrowly defined to absorb WIN-058b cleanly. Current definition (build-scripts/generate-html.js:1621): 'The claim relies on unreplicated fringe experiments or unverified data that has not been independently confirmed.' This fits WIN-011 (Mohe 1997 gravity) and WIN-015 (Meyl scalar Faraday) but fits WIN-058b (Bedrock Induction Ratio κ = 1.677, PRED-KAPPA-001) only loosely. WIN-058b's finding (data/wins.json:WIN-058b) describes post-hoc-fitted methodology and Monte Carlo showing r > 0.96 is unremarkable for shared EM forcing — these are Misleading-class problems (post-hoc fit, false significance) rather than the definition's 'unreplicated fringe experiment' framing. The c1 review flagged this same definitional narrowness and proposed broadening; no broadening has occurred. The verdict is still defensible because WIN-058b is a prospectively-registered prediction (PRED-KAPPA-001) awaiting independent confirmation, but the definition does not cleanly capture 'registered prospective prediction with methodologically suspect calibration awaiting independent test.'
holes_found.recommendation: Broaden the Not Demonstrated definition along the lines c1 suggested: 'The claim relies on unreplicated fringe experiments OR on prospectively-registered predictions whose calibration is methodologically suspect and which have not been independently verified.' This absorbs WIN-058b under a stated rule. Alternatively, restructure WIN-058b's finding to lead with the prospective-pending framing ('PRED-KAPPA-001 registered; awaiting independent verification') rather than the methodology critique ('post-hoc fit, r unremarkable'), and move the methodology critique to the detail.

holes_found.description: The 'b' suffix convention (WIN-057b, WIN-058b) is undocumented and creates reader confusion. data/wins.json:72 contains three WINs using the 'b' suffix: WIN-057b (Global cavity re-pressurization), WIN-058b (Bedrock Induction Ratio κ). The summary table (Part 3.1) renders these adjacent to WIN-057 (Two-zone disc topology) and WIN-058 (Unified angular coordinate) but the two are unrelated claims — the dome introduced new claims mid-cycle and we appended 'b' rather than renumbering or using a new ID. No documentation explains the convention. A reader scanning the summary table sees 'WIN-057 / WIN-057b' and naturally assumes they are related; in reality WIN-057b is a new dome claim (cavity re-pressurization) unrelated to WIN-057 (two-zone disc topology). Same for WIN-058 / WIN-058b.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a short note in the Part 3.1 Verdict Summary Table preamble or in the Evaluation Guide: 'WIN IDs with a `b` suffix (WIN-057b, WIN-058b) denote claims added by the dome after the original numbering was set; they share the dome's numbering convention but are independent claims, not refinements of their parent ID.' Alternatively, renumber to fresh sequential IDs (e.g., WIN-071, WIN-072) and add a redirect note for the original 057b/058b references. The first option is lower-effort and preserves cross-reference integrity.

holes_found.description: Std Model Explains category absorbed 'Non-discriminating' as a sub-pattern but the verdict legend (build-scripts/generate-html.js:1618) does not surface this. Legend text: 'The observation cited is real, but mainstream physics already provides a complete, quantitative explanation. The dome model adds no predictive power beyond what existing models already achieve.' This covers 'standard physics explains' but not the distinct sub-pattern several Std Model Explains findings now lead with — 'Non-discriminating: dome extrapolates same X as Y' (WIN-006, 010, 020, 025, 035, 036, 040, 043, 060, 068). Non-discrimination is a stronger criticism than 'standard physics explains' alone — it specifically claims the dome's prediction has zero discriminatory power between models, which the broader Std Model definition does not require. c1 flagged this conceptually; the legend still does not surface it.
holes_found.recommendation: Augment the Std Model Explains legend definition: 'The observation cited is real, but mainstream physics already provides a complete, quantitative explanation. The dome model adds no predictive power beyond what existing models already achieve. Some WINs in this category are flagged Non-discriminating, meaning the dome's prediction does not differ from the globe's prediction even in principle — failing the discriminating-prediction criterion in our Evaluation Guide.' This makes the sub-pattern visible without splitting the category.

holes_found.description: Verdict legend ordering (build-scripts/generate-html.js:1617-1622) is still not arranged by evidential force, repeating c1 hole. Current order: Refuted by Data → Standard Model Explains → Self-Contradicted → Misleading → Not Demonstrated → Unfalsifiable. A reader expects either severity-descending or grouped-by-failure-type. The natural grouping suggested in c1 (Wrong on own terms: Self-Contradicted, Refuted by Data; Not evidence for dome: Std Model Explains, Misleading; Not evaluable: Not Demonstrated, Unfalsifiable) makes the taxonomy's logical structure visible.
holes_found.recommendation: Reorder the legend (line 1617-1622 in generate-html.js) to: Self-Contradicted → Refuted by Data → Std Model Explains → Misleading → Not Demonstrated → Unfalsifiable. Group with subtle visual breaks (e.g., subtle horizontal rule between groups) to surface the three-tier structure. Pure cosmetic but improves comprehension. Carried forward from c1 unresolved.

holes_found.description: The Verdict Categories block (build-scripts/generate-html.js:1615, 'h1 id=verdicts') is the only h1-level definition block in Part 1 not wrapped in the <details><summary> + TLDR pattern used everywhere else in the document. This is a presentation inconsistency, not a substantive issue. Mobile/skim readers benefit from TLDRs throughout — the Verdict Categories block is exactly the kind of definitional content a skim-reader most needs a TLDR for.
holes_found.recommendation:
Wrap the Verdict Categories block in

Verdict Categories...

Six categories describe distinct failure modes: Self-Contradicted (dome's own math fails), Refuted by Data (measurement disproves the claim), Std Model Explains (mainstream physics already predicts the observation), Misleading (data mishandled or count inflated), Not Demonstrated (untested or unverified), Unfalsifiable (cannot be tested). See the Evaluation Guide for assignment criteria.

[current legend]
. Aligns with site-wide pattern.

stronger_arguments.description: Extend the Evaluation Guide's symmetric-counting acknowledgement (generate-html.js:1733) from tidal-cluster-only to all multi-WIN Self-Contradicted clusters. This is the highest-leverage taxonomic edit available — it preempts the strongest dome-defender process objection (counting hypocrisy) without changing any verdict assignment, and it strengthens the credibility of every other Self-Contradicted finding by demonstrating we apply our own counting standard symmetrically.
sources: Evaluation Guide 'On our counting methodology' paragraph at build-scripts/generate-html.js:1733
data/wins.json detail_group field (Schumann SC cluster currently NONE; cosmological SC cluster currently NONE)

stronger_arguments.description: Add a 'Non-discriminating' visual tag (not a new verdict, but a sub-label rendered alongside the Std Model Explains badge in the WIN summary table) for the 10 WINs whose findings explicitly use the word 'Non-discriminating' (WIN-006, 010, 020, 025, 035, 036, 040, 043, 060, 068). This surfaces the distinct sub-pattern without splitting the category, addressing the c1 'category overloaded' critique conservatively.
sources: data/wins.json verdicts:18 Std Model Explains, ~10 of which lead with 'Non-discriminating' in finding

stronger_arguments.description: Document the WIN ID numbering convention in Part 3.1 preamble or the Evaluation Guide. The current state — 72 WINs including 057b/058b suffixes — implies an editorial convention readers must reverse-engineer. A one-paragraph note prevents the most common misreading (assuming 057/057b are related claims).
sources: data/wins.json:72-total WINs with 057b and 058b using b-suffix
no documentation found in Part 3 preamble or Evaluation Guide

kernel_of_truth.description: The six-category taxonomy continues to capture the genuine diversity of failure modes in the dome model's claims (data/wins.json verdict distribution: 20 Self-Contradicted, 18 Std Model Explains, 16 Misleading, 12 Refuted by Data, 3 Not Demonstrated, 3 Unfalsifiable). The new Verdict Assignment Criteria section added since c1 (build-scripts/generate-html.js:1714-1736) directly addresses three of the four highest-severity holes c1 raised — the primary-issue rule for multi-failure WINs, the Misleading/Std Model decision boundary, and the rigged-taxonomy / no-positive-category objection. The latter is particularly well-handled: the 'On the absence of a Confirmed category' paragraph (generate-html.js:1735) explicitly states what would qualify and why none has been found, which is the right rhetorical move against the circularity objection. The Self-Contradicted category remains the structurally strongest verdict — internal-consistency contradictions cannot be rebutted by 'mainstream science is wrong' arguments.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The framework's conceptual strength does not transfer fully to application consistency. Three specific application gaps (Self-Contradicted cluster inflation in build-scripts/generate-html.js:1733, WIN-068 duplication-in-finding holdout in data/wins.json:WIN-068, undocumented b-suffix convention in data/wins.json:72) give a careful dome defender process objections at exactly the points the new Verdict Assignment Criteria section was designed to close. The conceptual taxonomy survives scrutiny; the per-WIN application still has visible inconsistencies.

specific_reclassification_recommendation.rationale: Verdict assignment is defensible (V12 0.9941 scale factor + WGS-84 input dependency are genuine Misleading-class issues), but the finding one-liner leads with non-discrimination ('relabeling of east-longitude convention') which mismatches the Misleading definition's required 'additional structural problem beyond non-discrimination'. Rewrite the finding to lead with the V12/V13 reconciliation failure.

specific_reclassification_recommendation.recommended_verdict: Not Demonstrated (keep) with definition broadening OR finding reframing
specific_reclassification_recommendation.rationale: Borderline. The verdict is defensible because PRED-KAPPA-001 is a prospectively-registered prediction awaiting independent verification — that is the 'unverified data' arm of the Not Demonstrated definition. But the finding leads with post-hoc-fit critique which reads as Misleading-class. Either broaden the Not Demonstrated definition (preferred — addresses c1 carry-over) or reframe the finding to lead with the prospective-pending framing.

specific_reclassification_recommendation.recommended_verdict: Std Model Explains (keep) with finding cleanup
specific_reclassification_recommendation.rationale: Verdict is correct per the primary-issue rule (Chapman–Sq framework is the primary explanation). But the finding's 'Duplicates WIN-010 and WIN-025 observational basis at a larger aggregation level' should move to detail_verdict_text to match the WIN-043/060 cleanup pattern. No verdict change needed; only the one-liner needs alignment.

missing_categories: No new missing categories since c1; 'Non-discriminating' is best addressed as a sub-label, not a 7th verdict
categories_that_should_split: Std Model Explains could surface a 'Non-discriminating' sub-label for the 10 WINs whose findings use that exact word, without splitting the category
taxonomy_coherence_assessment.explanation: Grade improved from B+ (c1) to A- (c2) because the new Verdict Assignment Criteria section (build-scripts/generate-html.js:1714-1736) closed the biggest c1 definitional holes (primary-issue rule, Misleading/Std Model boundary, no-positive-category circularity). Definitions are now clear and well-bounded. The grade does not reach A because three application-consistency gaps remain: Self-Contradicted cluster inflation undermines the symmetric-counting claim (the dome defender's strongest counter-rhetoric per the c1 review is now actively unanswered for 3 of 4 SC clusters); WIN-068 holds the only post-cleanup duplication-in-finding inconsistency; the b-suffix convention is undocumented. None is fatal; all three are fixable with text edits, not verdict changes. The structural foundation (six categories, primary-issue rule, internal-consistency standard) is sound.

deeper_analysis: The most important development since c1 is the addition of the 'Verdict Assignment Criteria' section to the Evaluation Guide (build-scripts/generate-html.js:1714-1736). This section answers — with stated rules, not implicit practice — the three biggest definitional objections a dome defender could raise: (1) 'How do you decide between Misleading and Std Model Explains?' Answered by the explicit decision-boundary paragraph (line 1720-1726). (2) 'How do you handle WINs that fail in multiple ways?' Answered by the primary-issue rule (line 1718). (3) 'Why is there no Confirmed category — isn't your taxonomy rigged?' Answered by the 'On the absence of a Confirmed category' paragraph (line 1735) with the explicit standard that would qualify. These are the three rebuttals that would have been hardest to recover from at c1. They are now substantively addressed at the definitional level. The remaining holes are all application-level inconsistencies — places where individual WIN findings don't follow the new rules consistently, or where the new rules don't quite cover an edge case (WIN-058b under the narrow Not Demonstrated definition). A counter-consideration from the other side: a dome defender could argue the new Verdict Assignment Criteria section is itself a hindsight rationalization — 'You wrote the assignment rules after the assignments were made, so the rules describe your decisions rather than constrain them.' This is a fair objection but not a damaging one, because the corrections that followed c1 (WIN-054 reclassification, WIN-043/060 duplication cleanup, WIN-023 archaeomagnetic kernel-of-truth) demonstrate the rules constrained subsequent revisions — they are not pure post-hoc description. The strongest remaining defender objection is the cluster-inflation argument (Hole #1 above): 'You apply the symmetric-counting standard to my tidal WINs but not to my Schumann or cosmological WINs.' This is the highest-leverage edit available because it preempts a critique the dome defender can construct from our own evidence. Counter-consideration on Hole #1 itself: it could be argued that recognizing more clusters would visually weaken the 20-Self-Contradicted headline number, which is rhetorically useful as-is. Reply: the rhetorical strength of 'X self-contradictions' depends on the reader accepting the count, and a sophisticated reader who notices the cluster pattern without our acknowledgement will discount the count silently. Better to acknowledge symmetrically and earn the credibility than to maximize the visible number and lose the careful reader. One more consideration: the verdict distribution shifted substantially since c1 (Self-Contradicted +67%, Misleading -30%) — this suggests the framework is being applied actively, not statically. That is a healthy sign. The clusters that grew (Self-Contradicted) are the most-defensible category. The categories that shrank (Misleading, Unfalsifiable) are the ones with the most contestable boundaries. Net: the migration was in the direction of taxonomic strength, not weakness.

data/wins.json:WIN-058 verdict='Misleading' with finding leading 'θ = −lonE is a relabeling'
data/wins.json:WIN-058b verdict='Not Demonstrated' with finding citing PRED-KAPPA-001
